heading: Feasibility study loan program
label: 1531 Feasibility study loan program
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (e), was repealed by , , .Section 1144 of this titlePub. L. 116–260, div. Z, title III, § 3002(i)(3)134 Stat. 2495Dec. 27, 2020
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (e), was repealed by , , .Section 1144 of this titlePub. L. 116–260, div. Z, title III, § 3002(i)(3)134 Stat. 2495Dec. 27, 2020

children.heading: Authorization; purposes
children.label: a Authorization; purposes
children.text: The Secretary is authorized and directed to establish a program of assistance for the accelerated development of geothermal resources for nonelectric applications by geothermal utility districts, geothermal industrial development districts, and other persons.

children.heading: Maximum amount of loan for costs of administration; cancellation of unpaid balance and accrued interest
children.label: b Maximum amount of loan for costs of administration; cancellation of unpaid balance and accrued interest

children.text: In providing assistance under the program established pursuant to subsection (a), the Secretary is authorized to make a loan to any person to defray up to 90 per centum of the costs of (A) studies to determine the feasibility of any geothermal development described in such subsection, and (B) preparing applications for any necessary licenses or other Federal, State, and local approvals respecting such development.

The Secretary may cancel the unpaid balance and any accrued interest on any loan granted for a study pursuant to clause (A) of paragraph (1) if he determines, on the basis of the study, that the geothermal development is not technically or economically feasible.

children.heading: Maximum amount of loan for costs of construction
children.label: c Maximum amount of loan for costs of construction

children.text: all necessary licenses and other required Federal, State, and local approvals for construction of such system or systems have been or will be issued,
the project involved will comply with all applicable laws relating to protection of the environment, and
the applicant requires such assistance to undertake and complete the project.

children.heading: Interest rate; term
children.label: d Interest rate; term
children.text: Each loan made pursuant to this section shall bear interest at a discount or interest rate equal to the rate in effect (at the time the loan is made) for water resources planning projects under section 80 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1974 (–17(a)). Each loan shall be for such term as the Secretary deems appropriate, but not in excess of ten years for loans under subsection (b) or thirty years for loans under subsection (c).42 U.S.C. 1962(d)1So in original. Should be “().”142 U.S.C. 1962d–17(a)

children.heading: Funding; deposit of amount repaid
children.label: e Funding; deposit of amount repaid
children.text: Loans pursuant to this section shall be made from funds appropriated (pursuant to this subchapter) to the Geothermal Resources Development Fund established under section 1144 of this title; and amounts repaid on such loans shall be deposited in the Geothermal Resources Development Fund for purposes of this subchapter.2See References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For loans under clause (A) of subsection (b)(1) for fiscal year 1981, there is authorized to be appropriated to the Geothermal Resources Development Fund not to exceed $5,000,000, which shall remain available until expended. For loans under such clause (A) for subsequent fiscal years, and for loans under clause (B) of subsection (b)(1) or under subsection (c) (for any such subsequent fiscal year), there may be appropriated to such Fund only such sums as are authorized by legislation hereafter enacted.

children.heading: “Person” defined
children.label: g “Person” defined
children.text: As used in this section, the term “person” includes municipalities, cooperatives, industrial development agencies, nonprofit organizations, and Indian tribes, as well as the districts referred to in subsection (a) and the other entities included within such term under .section 1 of title 1
